topic: percent markup and discount working backwards
latex:
In May, the price of a pair of jeans was 250\

}

\textbf{Answer:} Let $\$x$ be the wholesale cost. Then the cost in May was $\$2.5x$. The cost in June was $75\
\[\$.75(2.5x).\]The cost in July was half of that, but was also $\$22.50$, so
\[22.50=0.5(0.75(2.5x))=\frac12\cdot\frac34\cdot\frac52x=\frac{15x}{16}.\]Noticing a perfect square we see that $22.50=15\cdot 1.50$, so
\[1.50=\frac{x}{16}.\]This tells us $x=24$, so the jeans cost $\boxed{\$24}$.

As a check, note that the May price is $\$60$, the June price is $\$45$, and the July price is $\$22.50$, as required.
